# Pinball Map API

Pinball Map (pinballmap.com) is a crowdsourced directory of pinball machines at locations worldwide. This document is for AI coding assistants helping developers build apps against this API.

Full interactive documentation: https://pinballmap.com/api/v1/docs

## Base URL and Format

All API endpoints are under: `https://pinballmap.com/api/v1/`

Append `.json` to any endpoint (e.g. `GET /api/v1/locations.json`). All responses are JSON.

## Authentication

Read-only (GET) endpoints are public — no auth required.

Write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE) require a user account. Pass credentials as query params on every write request:

```
user_email=user@example.com&user_token=abc123
```

Obtain a token by calling `GET /api/v1/users/auth_details.json?login=user@example.com&password=secret`. The response includes `authentication_token`. Store it; do not re-fetch the token on every request.

## Critical: Request Volume Anti-Patterns

**If your app is making hundreds of requests, it is almost certainly designed incorrectly.**

The API provides bulk endpoints that return many records in a single response. There is no legitimate use case that requires looping over individual location, machine, or LMX records one at a time.

Examples of broken patterns and their correct replacements:

| Wrong (N+1) | Correct (one request) |
|---|---|
| Loop calling `GET /locations/:id` for each location | `GET /locations.json?region=portland` — returns all locations in a region |
| Loop calling `GET /locations/:id` with machine filter | `GET /locations.json?by_machine_id=41` — returns all locations with that machine |
| Loop calling `GET /location_machine_xrefs/:id` for each LMX | `GET /region/:region/location_machine_xrefs.json` — returns all LMXs for a region |
| Loop calling `GET /machine_score_xrefs/:id` for each score | `GET /machine_score_xrefs/highest.json` — returns highest score per machine |

A well-designed client typically makes fewer than 10 API calls for a full app startup sequence.

## Rate Limiting

Two layers of rate limiting are in place: Rack::Attack (IP-based) and per-endpoint controller limits. Exceeding a limit returns HTTP 429. These limits exist to prevent abuse, not to restrict normal usage — a well-designed app will never come close to them.

| Endpoint | Limit |
|---|---|
| `POST /location_machine_xrefs` (add machine) | no controller limit |
| `PUT /location_machine_xrefs/:id` (update condition) | 50 per 10 minutes |
| `DELETE /location_machine_xrefs/:id` (remove machine) | 100 per 10 minutes |
| `GET /region/:region/location_machine_xrefs` (bulk fetch) | 120 per minute |
| `PUT /locations/:id` (update location) | 30 per 10 minutes |
| `POST /locations/suggest` | 30 per 10 minutes |
| `PUT /machine_conditions/:id` | 50 per 5 minutes |
| `POST /machine_score_xrefs` (submit score) | 80 per 2 minutes |
| `POST /location_picture_xrefs` (upload photo) | 20 per 2 minutes |
| `GET /users/auth_details`, `POST /users/signup`, `POST /users/forgot_password`, `POST /users/resend_confirmation` | 40 per 5 minutes |
| `GET /users/:id/profile_info` | 40 per 2 minutes |

## Reducing Response Size: no_details

Several endpoints accept a `no_details` param that strips fields you likely don't need. **Start with `no_details=1` and only fetch the full payload if you actually need the omitted fields.**

### no_details on location endpoints

| | Full (default) | no_details=1 | no_details=2 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Location fields omitted | none | `phone`, `website`, `description`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `date_last_updated`, `last_updated_by_user_id`, `region_id`, `users_count`, `user_submissions_count` | all of level 1, plus `street`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `updated_at`, `location_type_id`, `operator_id` — essentially just ID, name, lat, lon |
| Machine data | full LMX list with machine conditions and scores | machine names + count, no conditions | none (within_bounding_box) or machine names without year (show) |

The `GET /locations/:id.json` show endpoint also accepts `metadata_only=1`, which returns the location record with no LMX list at all — useful when you only need to refresh location metadata.

### no_details on other endpoints

**`GET /machines.json`** — any truthy value omits `is_active`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `ipdb_link`, `ipdb_id`, `machine_display`, `kineticist_url`. Recommended for app startup: you rarely need those fields when building a machine list.

**`GET /operators.json`** — `no_details=1` omits contact fields and timestamps, adds an `operator_has_email` boolean. `no_details=2` also omits `website`.

### Which endpoints support no_details

- `GET /api/v1/locations.json` — any value
- `GET /api/v1/locations/:id.json` — values `1` and `2` (also `metadata_only=1`)
- `GET /api/v1/locations/within_bounding_box.json` — values `1` and `2`
- `GET /api/v1/locations/closest_by_lat_lon.json` — any value
- `GET /api/v1/locations/closest_by_address.json` — any value
- `GET /api/v1/machines.json` — any value
- `GET /api/v1/operators.json` — values `1` and `2`

## Endpoints

### Locations

```
GET /api/v1/locations.json
GET /api/v1/region/:region/locations.json
```

The primary bulk endpoint. Accepts many filter params (all optional, combinable):

- `by_location_name` — search by venue name
- `by_location_id` — single location by ID (use the show endpoint instead if you only need one)
- `by_machine_id` — locations with any variant of this machine
- `by_machine_single_id` — locations with exactly this machine version
- `by_machine_group_id` — locations with any machine in this group
- `by_machine_id_ic` / `by_machine_single_id_ic` — same as above but only Insider Connected
- `by_city_id` / `by_city_no_state` — filter by city
- `by_state_name` / `by_state_id` — filter by state
- `by_country` — filter by country
- `by_type_id` — filter by location type (bar, arcade, etc.)
- `by_zone_id` — filter by zone
- `by_operator_id` — filter by operator
- `by_at_least_n_machines` — minimum machine count
- `manufacturer` — filter by machine manufacturer
- `by_machine_year`, `by_machine_year_gte`, `by_machine_year_lte` — filter by machine manufacture year
- `by_is_stern_army` — Stern Army locations only (**requires** `no_details` to be set)
- `by_ic_active` — Insider Connected active
- `regionless_only` — locations not assigned to any region

```
GET /api/v1/locations/:id.json             # single location
GET /api/v1/locations/:id/machine_details.json   # machines at this location
GET /api/v1/locations/:id/picture_details.json   # photos at this location
PUT /api/v1/locations/:id.json             # update metadata (auth required)
PUT /api/v1/locations/:id/confirm.json     # confirm info is current (auth required)
```

Geographic search:
```
GET /api/v1/locations/closest_by_lat_lon.json?lat=45.52&lon=-122.68&max_distance=10
GET /api/v1/locations/closest_by_address.json?address=1234+Main+St+Portland+OR
GET /api/v1/locations/within_bounding_box.json?swlat=45.4&swlon=-122.8&nelat=45.6&nelon=-122.5
```

Stats / utility:
```
GET /api/v1/locations/top_cities.json
GET /api/v1/locations/top_cities_by_machine.json
GET /api/v1/locations/top_locations.json
GET /api/v1/locations/type_count.json
GET /api/v1/locations/countries.json
GET /api/v1/locations/autocomplete.json?name=ground+kontrol
GET /api/v1/locations/autocomplete_city.json?name=portland
GET /api/v1/locations/geocode_lat_lon.json?address=1234+Main+St+Portland+OR  # see note below
POST /api/v1/locations/suggest.json        # submit a new location (auth required)
```

**Note on `geocode_lat_lon`:** This endpoint calls a third-party geocoding service that incurs a cost per request. Use it sparingly — only when a user explicitly provides an address that needs to be converted to coordinates. Do not call it in bulk, in loops, or speculatively.

### Machines

```
GET /api/v1/machines.json      # all machines in the database
GET /api/v1/machines/:id.json  # single machine
```

### Machine Groups

Machine groups link variants/versions of the same title together.

```
GET /api/v1/machine_groups.json      # all groups
GET /api/v1/machine_groups/:id.json  # single group
```

### Location Machine Xrefs (LMX)

An LMX represents a specific machine installed at a specific location.

```
GET /api/v1/region/:region/location_machine_xrefs.json   # all LMXs for a region
GET /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/:id.json              # single LMX
POST /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs.json                 # add machine to location (auth required)
PUT /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/:id.json              # update condition (auth required)
DELETE /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/:id.json           # remove machine (auth required)
PUT /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/:id/ic_toggle.json    # toggle Insider Connected (auth required)
GET /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/top_n_machines.json   # most-installed machines globally
GET /api/v1/location_machine_xrefs/most_recent_by_lat_lon.json?lat=45.52&lon=-122.68
```

### Machine Conditions

Condition notes are attached to LMXs, not directly to machines.

```
PUT /api/v1/machine_conditions/:id.json     # update condition note (auth required)
DELETE /api/v1/machine_conditions/:id.json  # remove condition note (auth required)
```

### Machine Score Xrefs (High Scores)

```
GET /api/v1/machine_score_xrefs/highest.json              # highest score per machine globally
GET /api/v1/region/:region/machine_score_xrefs.json       # all scores for a region
GET /api/v1/machine_score_xrefs/:id.json                  # single score record
POST /api/v1/machine_score_xrefs.json                     # submit a score (auth required)
PUT /api/v1/machine_score_xrefs/:id.json                  # update a score (auth required)
DELETE /api/v1/machine_score_xrefs/:id.json               # delete a score (auth required)
```

### Regions

A region is a geographic grouping of locations (e.g. "portland", "chicago"). The `:region` path param is the lowercase region name.

```
GET /api/v1/regions.json
GET /api/v1/regions/:id.json
GET /api/v1/regions/closest_by_lat_lon.json?lat=45.52&lon=-122.68
GET /api/v1/regions/does_region_exist.json?name=portland
GET /api/v1/regions/location_and_machine_counts.json
POST /api/v1/regions/suggest.json     # suggest a new region
POST /api/v1/regions/contact.json     # contact a region admin
```

### Users

```
GET /api/v1/users/auth_details.json?login=user@example.com&password=secret
POST /api/v1/users/signup.json
POST /api/v1/users/forgot_password.json
POST /api/v1/users/resend_confirmation.json
GET /api/v1/users/total_user_count.json
GET /api/v1/users/:id/profile_info.json
GET /api/v1/users/:id/list_fave_locations.json    (auth required)
POST /api/v1/users/:id/add_fave_location.json     (auth required)
POST /api/v1/users/:id/remove_fave_location.json  (auth required)
GET /api/v1/users/:id/render_user_flag.json
POST /api/v1/users/:id/update_user_flag.json      (auth required)
```

### User Submissions (Activity Feed)

```
GET /api/v1/user_submissions.json                        # global feed
GET /api/v1/region/:region/user_submissions.json         # region feed
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/location.json?id=:location_id
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/list_within_range.json?lat=45.52&lon=-122.68&max_distance=10
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/delete_location.json        # recently removed locations
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/top_users.json
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/total_user_submission_count.json
GET /api/v1/user_submissions/total_user_submission_count_week.json
```

The index, `location`, and `list_within_range` endpoints accept these optional filter params:

- `submission_type` — filter by activity type. Multiple values: `submission_type[]=remove_machine&submission_type[]=new_lmx`
- `machine_id` — filter by machine. Multiple values: `machine_id[]=111&machine_id[]=222`
- `user_id` — limit to a single user (by ID)
- `user_name` — limit to a single user (by name)
- `user_faved` — limit to locations favorited by this user ID
- `limit` — cap result count and include pagination metadata in the response
- `restrict_to` — restrict a specific submission type to the `user_id` user; if `user_id` is absent, that type is excluded entirely. Used by the app to show scores only from the current user.

`list_within_range` also accepts:
- `min_date_of_submission` — earliest date to include, format `YYYY-MM-DD`
- `region_id` — limit to a region (by ID, not name)

### Location Pictures

```
GET /api/v1/location_picture_xrefs/:id.json
POST /api/v1/location_picture_xrefs.json   (auth required)
DELETE /api/v1/location_picture_xrefs/:id.json  (auth required)
```

### Reference / Lookup Tables

These return small, stable datasets. Fetch once at startup and cache locally.

```
GET /api/v1/location_types.json
GET /api/v1/location_types/:id.json
GET /api/v1/statuses.json
GET /api/v1/operators.json
GET /api/v1/region/:region/operators.json
GET /api/v1/region/:region/zones.json
GET /api/v1/region/:region/region_link_xrefs.json
```

## Do Not Do These Things

- Do not loop over `/api/v1/locations/:id` to build a list of locations. Use the index endpoint with filters.
- Do not poll the API repeatedly to detect changes. There is no webhook system; build your own sync schedule with reasonable intervals.
- Do not fetch `auth_details` (login) on every API call. Fetch the token once, store it, reuse it.
- Do not use undocumented endpoints or URL patterns not listed here — they are web-only routes and will not behave like an API.
- Do not make requests against `/:region/locations` (the web route) — use `/api/v1/region/:region/locations.json`.
